layers.title: Register logs command
layers.summary: The CLI command registry is extended so `dscli logs` can be discovered alongside the existing lifecycle commands. This is an additive command registration with no removal or signature change to existing command entries.

layers.title: Implement log tailing
layers.summary: Building on registration, the new command validates a local worker address and line count, resolves the target worker by its `-worker_address=` process argument, derives the INFO log path, and shells out to `tail -n`. The implementation is read-only against worker state, but it touches operator diagnostics and local process inspection paths.
layers.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,178 @@
+"""``dscli logs`` subcommand: print recent log lines for a worker service."""
+
+_DEFAULT_LINES = 100
+_WORKER_ADDRESS_RE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9.-]+:[0-9]+$")
+
+class Command(BaseCommand):
+    """Print the last N lines of a worker's log file to stdout."""
+
+    name = "logs"
+    description = "print recent log lines for a worker service"
+
+    def run(self, args):
+        """Print the tail of the worker's log to stdout."""
+        address = _validate_worker_address(args.worker_address)
+        pid = self.find_worker_pid(address)
+        log_path = self.resolve_log_path(pid)
+        result = subprocess.run(
+            ["tail", "-n", str(lines), log_path], capture_output=True, text=True,
+        )

layers.title: Cover command paths
layers.summary: The tests exercise the main success path, explicit worker log flags, and input rejection before invoking `tail`. This validates the new command surface without changing product runtime behavior or persisted state.
layers.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,106 @@
+"""Unit tests for the ``dscli logs`` subcommand."""
+
+class TestLogsCommand(unittest.TestCase):
+    """Tests for :class:`yr.datasystem.cli.logs.Command`."""
+
+    def test_run_prints_recent_lines_from_default_worker_info_log(
+        self, _mock_pid, mock_run, _mock_args, mock_defaults
+    ):
+        """A running worker's log tail is printed and the command succeeds."""
+
+    def test_run_uses_worker_cmdline_log_config(
+        self, _mock_pid, mock_run, _mock_args, mock_defaults
+    ):
+        """Explicit worker log flags select the current INFO log file."""
+
+    def test_run_rejects_invalid_worker_address(self, mock_run):
+        """A malformed worker address is rejected before running tail."""

layers.title: Document operator workflow
layers.summary: The design and implementation plan describe the intended local, read-only workflow and the excluded follow or aggregation modes. They also state the verification command expected for the new test file.
layers.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+# `dscli logs` Subcommand — Implementation Plan
+
+1. **Add `cli/logs.py`** — a `Command(BaseCommand)` subclass:
+   - `run(args)`: resolve the worker by address, locate its log file under the
+     worker log directory, and print the last N lines to stdout.
+
+## Verification
+
+- `python -m unittest cli.tests.test_logs` passes.
@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+# `dscli logs` Subcommand — Design Spec
+
+Add a read-only `dscli logs` subcommand that prints the last N lines of a
+datasystem worker's log file, selected by the worker's address.

summary: This PR adds a read-only `dscli logs` command that locates a local worker by address and prints the tail of its INFO log, with unit tests and design notes.
